response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist web/full-stack engineer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. Hands-on C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of Windows C++ development. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience (MongoDB/MySQL only), and no fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain exposure; RIDE Capital was a Node.js/TypeScript retail-investment product, not investment banking. Optional pluses like CI/CD and scripting do not offset the missing core requirements.
strengths: Broad, versatile engineering background across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Solid software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, and open-source contributions
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (DLang microservices at Dunnhumby)
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, and none on the Windows platform stack required
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
No fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and testing fundamentals
